folio.label: 160v
folio.l: ¢ Gladly q¶ I , by goddes swete pyne
I wol ©ow telle , a litil thyngö in prose
That ougúte like ©ow , as I suppose
Or elles certes , ©e be to daungerous
It is a moral tale , vertuous
Al be it told , somtyme , in sondry wyse
Of sondry folk , as I shal ©ow deuyse
As thus , ©e wot that euery Eungelist
That telletú vs , the peyne of Ihu~ crist
Ne seitú nat alle thyngö , as his felawe dotú
But nathelees , his sentence is al sotú
And alle accorden , as in here sentence
Al be there , in here tellyngö difference
For so~me of hem seyn more , & some sey lasse
Whan they , his pitous passiou~ expresse
I mene of Mark , Mathew , Luke and Ioún
But doutelees , here sentence is al oon
Therfore lordynggã alle , I ©ow byseche
If ©ow thynke I varie , as in my speche
As thus , thougú that I telle , somwhat more
Of prouerbes , than ©e han herd bifore
Comprehended , in this litel tretys heere
To enforce witú , the effect of my matere
And thougú I nat , the same wordes seye
As ©e han herd , ©et to ©ow alle I preye
Blametú me nougút , for as in my sentence
Shuln ©e no wher , fynden , difference
Fro the sentence , of this tretys lite
After the whicú , this mery tale I wryte
And æäfore herkenetú , what that I shal seye
And lat me tellen al my tale , I preye
¢ Here endetú Chaucers tale of Thopas , & the ¸loge of Melibeus
